caption.text: We started with an underutilized UX shortcut, but one that significantly
increases your retention rate.
Asymmetric celebration. Or in other words,
celebrate differently based on the importance of the action.
Most SaaS platforms celebrate all actions in the same way,
a small toast
"Action saved." It's flat, it creates no emotion and no attachment. Imagine,
your user has just finalized their first complete project.
The classic scenario is a discreet little toast in the top right corner
"project saved." They return to the dashboard,
they move on to something else, there's no emotion.
Now,
imagine the same scenario but this time with a full-screen animation,
like confetti for example.
And there it says
"Congratulations, you just finished your first project,
you just saved 3 hours of work" with a CTA
"Create your next project in 2 clicks"
This second scenario,
believe me, it changes quite a few things.
First, because you create emotional recognition,
your user feels valued,
they become attached to the product.
Second, because you quantify the impact.
3 hours saved provides concrete proof of value. And third, because you
capitalize on the
current motivation, they just succeeded,
they are motivated and you suggest they start again immediately.
This little UX shortcut you can use with a simple formula:
Minor action,
discreet toast. Medium action,
confirmation +
suggestion for next step. And major action,
visual celebration + quantified impact + CTA. Result,
your user no longer sees your SaaS as a tool,
they see it as a partner that celebrates their victories.
So identify the 3 most
important actions of your SaaS and create a celebration for each.
